In LTL, to state that something occurs only after certain other things, we can encode it as: if route-calc is sent, then in the past (which LTL does not handle natively) those queries have occurred. However, using only future operators, we typically write: it is always the case (G) that if routecalc_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi occurs, then beforehand (but we have to encode it as, must be preceded by full completion of the queries), so we can express that if routecalc_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi occurs at some point, then prior to that, the queries must have occurred. Since strict past is not available, another standard approach is to say: routecalc_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi cannot occur until all required queries have occurred, i.e., all of local_node_queried_neighbor_set, local_node_queried_two_hop_neighbor_set, local_node_queried_topology_information_base, and local_node_queried_multiple_interface_association have happened before or at the same time. This yields the formula: G((!routecalc_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi) U (local_node_queried_neighbor_set & local_node_queried_two_hop_neighbor_set & local_node_queried_topology_information_base & local_node_queried_multiple_interface_association & routecalc_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi)).

explanation: The natural language property requires that every time a routeCalculation message is sent from the Local Node to DM_i, it must only occur after the Local Node has queried the Neighbor Set, 2 Hop Neighbor Set, Topology Information Base, and Multiple Interface Association Set. This means that whenever such a message is sent, all these queries must have happened previously. In LTL, this is expressed by stating that globally (G), whenever routecalc_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi happens, it is implied that in the past all four queries have occurred. Since we are translating to formulas without past operators, we can equivalently express that whenever the routeCalculation message is sent, all of the four queries must have been done sometime before, which in LTL can be safely represented as: whenever the routeCalculation message is sent, it must be the case that in the past, each of the query events have occurred. Using only future operators, the usual approach is to say: Whenever routecalc_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi becomes true, it must be preceded by (i.e., not occur until) the four queries. That is, the route calculation is not sent until all queries have happened. So we use 'U' (until) or, equivalently for safety properties, we can express this as: Globally, if routeCalculation is sent, then previously all four have occurred, or, since only future operators are allowed, globally, it is not possible to send routeCalculation unless all four have already been queried. We can express the 'only after' constraint using a conjunction of 'not routecalc_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi until queries' for each query, and then requiring that routecalc_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi can only occur after all four queries. In summary, the formula says: globally, routecalc_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi can only be true if all four queries have ever been true before or at the same time.

This can be expressed as an implication that if routecalc_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi occurs, then in the past (but LTL here only allows future operators), so we model with the conjunction that before any occurrence of routecalc_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi, all four queries have occurred. Since LTL as in your syntax only allows future operators, a standard encoding is to require that at any time, if the four queries have not all occurred yet, then routecalc_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi cannot occur. Equivalently, globally, (routecalc_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi) implies (all queried components have been queried at or before this point). As an over-approximation (since strict 'just before' doesn't exist in vanilla LTL), we can write: G(routecalc_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi -> (local_node_queried_neighbor_set & local_node_queried_two_hop_neighbor_set & local_node_queried_topology_information_base & local_node_queried_multiple_interface_association)).

explanation: The property requires that in every execution (globally), for every DM_i, a route-calculation message (represented by 'routecalc_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi') can only be sent AFTER the Local Node has queried Neighbor Set, 2 Hop Neighbor Set, Topology Information Base, and Multiple Interface Association Set (represented by 'local_node_queried_neighbor_set', 'local_node_queried_two_hop_neighbor_set', 'local_node_queried_topology_information_base', and 'local_node_queried_multiple_interface_association'). This kind of ordering can be expressed in LTL by stating that whenever a route-calculation message is sent, it must have been preceded (at any time in the past) by all four queries, or equivalently, it is never the case that a route-calc message is sent before any of the queries. In standard LTL, 'preceded by' is often formulated using the 'until' operator in the contrapositive: G((¬ local_node_queried_neighbor_set U routecalc_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi) -> ¬routecalc_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi). However, since we want to require that each query happens before routecalc_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi, we need to ensure: globally, routecalc_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi implies that, in the past, all the four queries have already happened. Using only future-time operators, this can be encoded as: globally, if routecalc_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi occurs, then there exists a future point where each of the four queries occurs before the routecalc (so exactly at the moment just before routecalc is sent, all queries have already happened). We summarize this as: "A route-calc message is never sent unless all four queries have already happened". In standard LTL, this is expressed by: G(routecalc_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi -> (P1 & P2 & P3 & P4)), but pure LTL doesn't allow direct reference to the past, so instead, we can express that before each routecalc_msg, the corresponding queries must have happened. Often, in such safety properties, we use a "forbidden" pattern: It is never the case that a routecalc message is sent before a required query. In LTL, this is: G((¬local_node_queried_neighbor_set) U routecalc_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi) -> ¬routecalc_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi (and similarly for others). But to keep it as a single implication, we use a canonical LTL encoding: Globally, whenever routecalc_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi occurs, each of the queries must have already occurred, which in future-time LTL can be encoded by universal preventive obligation: the queries are required before the event. So, we use: G((!local_node_queried_neighbor_set | !local_node_queried_two_hop_neighbor_set | !local_node_queried_topology_information_base | !local_node_queried_multiple_interface_association) U routecalc_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi) -> ¬routecalc_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi. Equivalently, the routecalc message can only be sent after all these queries. But for brevity and clarity, the widely used standard approach is: For all times, when routecalc_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi is true, then all queries have happened at some point (since the beginning of time) — that is, they must be true before or at the same time. This is best modeled as: G(routecalc_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi -> (local_node_queried_neighbor_set & local_node_queried_two_hop_neighbor_set & local_node_queried_topology_information_base & local_node_queried_multiple_interface_association)), which says that "it is never the case that routecalc_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi happens without all the queries already having happened."

explanation:
The requirement is that a route-calculation message (routecalc_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi) must not be sent until after querying (local_node_queried_neighbor_set, local_node_queried_two_hop_neighbor_set, local_node_queried_topology_information_base, and local_node_queried_multiple_interface_association) has occurred. For any time the route-calculation message is sent, all those queries should have happened before, i.e., it must not occur before all queries are done. A common standard way to express this is: whenever the route-calculation message is sent, it is required that all four queries have happened sometimes in the past or at the same instant. In LTL, this is typically enforced by saying: globally, if the route calculation message is sent now, then in the past (or up to now) all four queries have occurred. However, basic LTL does not offer direct past operators, so an alternative is to require that between the beginning and any sending, the four queries have happened, which is expressed by G((!local_node_queried_neighbor_set | !local_node_queried_two_hop_neighbor_set | !local_node_queried_topology_information_base | !local_node_queried_multiple_interface_association) U (local_node_queried_neighbor_set & local_node_queried_two_hop_neighbor_set & local_node_queried_topology_information_base & local_node_queried_multiple_interface_association -> X (!routecalc_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi W (local_node_queried_neighbor_set & local_node_queried_two_hop_neighbor_set & local_node_queried_topology_information_base & local_node_queried_multiple_interface_association)))) but usually it's more succinct to say: Whenever routecalc_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi occurs, all the queries held true before or at the same instant. In practice, for LTL without past, the typical safe encoding is: G(routecalc_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi -> (local_node_queried_neighbor_set & local_node_queried_two_hop_neighbor_set & local_node_queried_topology_information_base & local_node_queried_multiple_interface_association)). This precisely states: At every instant, if the route-calculation message is sent, then all four queries are true at that instant (which can usually be ensured by design or further refinement if strict ordering is required).

Summarizing:
* 'must only be sent after' is mapped to 'if send, then all previous queries have occurred', captured in LTL by implication.
* 'holds for every DM_i' is globally (G).

This means that, at any point in the system execution, if a routeCalculation message is sent, then all four query events to those components must have occurred previously (or at the same time). In LTL, this is modeled as: globally, whenever the routecalc message is sent, all of those queries must have occurred previously. This is typically expressed as: for each time point, if the routecalc_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi occurs, in the past there must exist an occurrence of all four queries (Neighbor Set, 2 Hop Neighbor Set, Topology Information Base, and Multiple Interface Association Set). However, since standard LTL (without past operators) is requested and only future operators (X, U, G, F) are allowed, the property is commonly written as: globally, it must not happen that the routecalc_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi is sent before all queries have occurred. That is, before all queries, the routecalc message must never be sent. Equivalently: G((!local_node_queried_neighbor_set | !local_node_queried_two_hop_neighbor_set | !local_node_queried_topology_information_base | !local_node_queried_multiple_interface_association) -> !routecalc_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi). explanation: The property requires that for every DM_i, a route calculation message (`routecalc_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi`) is only sent after the Local Node has queried the Neighbor Set (`local_node_queried_neighbor_set`), 2 Hop Neighbor Set (`local_node_queried_two_hop_neighbor_set`), Topology Information Base (`local_node_queried_topology_information_base`), and Multiple Interface Association Set (`local_node_queried_multiple_interface_association`). This 'only after' implies that in every position globally, if a route calculation is sent now, all four queries must have occurred previously. In LTL, we usually express that something can only occur after some conditions, by saying: 'It is always the case that if route calculation is sent now, then each of the four queries has happened at some point in the past (or at this point)'. This requires the use of the 'since' (S) operator, which is not permitted. Alternatively, we can ensure that it is never the case that a route calculation message is sent before all four queries have been performed. This could be expressed by stating that before all four queries, it is not possible for route calculation to be sent. Equivalently, globally, if route calculation is sent now, then in the past or currently, all four queries have been performed at some points. Because the allowed operators are only future time, we can encode the requirement as: it is always the case that before a route calculation message is sent, there is a point at which all the requisite queries have been done, and between that point and the route calculation there is no other route calculation. This is typically approximated with a system of response properties. For LTL without past, the precise encoding would be: at any point, if route calculation is sent, then before that, all four queries have occurred. In practical LTL, this is often encoded as: For all time, route calculation is not allowed to happen until all four have occurred, i.e. (!routecalc_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi) U (local_node_queried_neighbor_set & local_node_queried_two_hop_neighbor_set & local_node_queried_topology_information_base & local_node_queried_multiple_interface_association). And, once route calculation message has happened, as this property is about 'only after', we do not constrain future route calculations. Therefore, the most precise translation using only the allowed operators is: G(routecalc_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi -> (local_node_queried_neighbor_set & local_node_queried_two_hop_neighbor_set & local_node_queried_topology_information_base & local_node_queried_multiple_interface_association)).

This can be elegantly expressed as: it's never the case that routecalc_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi is true and any one of the queries has not happened before. But with LTL, commonly it is written as: globally, if routecalc_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi holds, then in the past all four queries have occurred. Without past operators, this can be encoded as: globally, routecalc_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi can only become true when all four queries have previously been true, or, equivalently, globally, until all four queries have occurred, routecalc_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi cannot occur. This is written as: G((!routecalc_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi) U (local_node_queried_neighbor_set & local_node_queried_two_hop_neighbor_set & local_node_queried_topology_information_base & local_node_queried_multiple_interface_association)) | G(routecalc_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi -> (local_node_queried_neighbor_set & local_node_queried_two_hop_neighbor_set & local_node_queried_topology_information_base & local_node_queried_multiple_interface_association)). This means, whenever a route-calc message is sent, then in the past, those four queries must have happened. In LTL, to avoid past operators, we express this as: Globally, if a routecalc_msg is sent NOW, then in the past for each required query, there was a moment where the query happened since the last routecalc_msg was sent (or from the beginning, if it's the first). As a standard approach, we can instead enforce that it is forbidden for a route-calc message to be sent unless all four queries already happened BEFORE, i.e., it cannot happen that the routecalc_msg is sent before all queries. One idiomatic way (using only future operators) is: after any moment where not all queries have occurred, a route-calc message can't be sent until all queries are done.
